section_miscategorizations.text: The doctor observes limited neck range of motion.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Correctly placed as an exam finding.
This is a patient-reported symptom and belongs in the history, not in objective findings.
Back pain is a subjective symptom reported by the patient and should be in the HPI.
Bruising could be either reported or observed; here it is explicitly framed as patient-reported, so it more properly belongs in HPI, though this is not highly misleading.
Swelling is framed as a patient report rather than an observed exam finding, so it fits better in HPI, but the misplacement is not strongly misleading.
Dizziness is a subjective symptom and should be documented in the HPI rather than in objective findings.
Headache is a subjective symptom and belongs in the HPI section.
Numbness is a subjective neurologic symptom and should be in the HPI, not in objective findings.
This describes an intended clinical action (ongoing evaluation of pain) rather than a diagnostic impression; it fits better as part of the plan, though the meaning is not substantially distorted.
